name: Hawaiian Brian's
tagline: A 21,000 sqft entertainment complex on Kapiolani — 24 pool tables, live music stages, a speakeasy, and arcade games since 1983.
mustOrder: a round of pool — 24 tables means you never wait long
catch a local band at The Studio or Crossroads venues
cheap beers at any of the multiple bars
parking: Building parking on Kapiolani Blvd (above 24 Hour Fitness)
talkStory: Hawaiian Brian's (now HB Social Club) has been a Honolulu institution since 1983, originally on Keeaumoku before moving to Kapiolani Blvd. The scale is staggering: 21,000 square feet across 24 pool tables, 10+ dart boards, two concert venues (The Studio and Crossroads), a speakeasy called Slack Key Lounge, and a full arcade. It's where locals go for a cheap night out — shoot pool, catch a local band, play darts, drink affordable beers. 18+ venue. Not a dive bar despite the vibe; more like Honolulu's living room for nightlife that doesn't involve a dress code.
